column3: Ready / under-construction flat, or plot self-build.
Drives UDS, depreciation, premium, maintenance treatment.
LetOut (earns rent) or SelfOccupied (carrying cost only).
Where surplus rent goes: ReinvestEquity / PrepayLoan / Pocket.
India_Old allows let-out loss set-off; India_New strands it.
SameCashSIP invests each EMI as an equity SIP; LumpsumOnly does not.

column6: Definition
Saleable area incl. common-area share — the basis builders quote and that structure value scales with.
Usable floor area within walls. Display/sanity only unless rent is per-carpet-sqft.
Undivided share of land your flat owns. Drives land value — higher UDS = more land beta. ≈ full plot for self-build.
Structure age at t=0. 0 for a new build/new apartment; older = more depreciation.
Apartment price (BSP + PLC + floor rise + parking + amenities), excl. stamp/reg/GST. For a plot, the LAND price only.
Market rent at t=0 if let out. For a plot, the rent at completion (grown from t=0 thereafter).
Annual market rent growth in years 1–5.
Annual market rent growth in years 6–10.
Annual market rent growth in years 11–20.
Rent growth lost as the building ages relative to newer stock, phased in after year 10.
Share of annual rent lost to vacant months.
Months of rent paid as brokerage on each re-let; annualized over a ~3-year tenancy.
Government transfer tax. Bangalore ~7% (2% registration since 31 Aug 2025); Mumbai ~6–7%.
Applies to under-construction property only; 0 for ready/OC-received.
Agent commission on purchase.
Assessor/valuation + legal due diligence + documentation + khata/mutation transfer.
Initial fit-out (modular kitchen, wardrobes, fittings). For a plot, the built house's interiors.
Current market land rate per sq ft, applied to UDS. The appreciating part of the asset.
Annual land-value growth, years 1–10.
Annual land-value growth, years 11–20 (usually lower as the micro-market matures).
Cost to rebuild the structure today, per sq ft. Inflates over time but the structure itself depreciates.
Annual growth in rebuild cost (materials + labour).
Straight-line wear of the structure (≈1.67%/yr for a 60-yr RCC life).
Obsolescence on top of physical wear (dated layouts, amenities) — higher for tall societies.
Minimum fraction of new value the structure retains, however old (e.g. 0.10).
Brand/newness premium buyers pay for a fresh building, per sq ft. ~0 for self-build.
Years over which the newness premium fades to zero.
Monthly common-area maintenance per sq ft charged by the society.
Owner-borne recurring upkeep (repairs the tenant doesn't cover), as a share of annual rent.
Annual increase in CAM/maintenance charges.
Extra upkeep growth that compounds with building age (cracks, waterproofing, lift overhauls).
Annual municipal property tax at t=0.
Annual growth in the property-tax bill.
Separate water/borewell/tanker charge — material for plots/villas; usually inside CAM for flats (0).
Sinking-fund accrual for big repairs (roof, structure) the owner bears even when the tenant pays CAM.
How often interiors are redone (kitchen, paint, fittings). 0 turns it off.
Cost of each refresh as a share of the (inflated) initial fit-out.
Principal borrowed (apartment). Down payment = price − loan + entry costs. For a plot, the land loan.
Annual home-loan interest rate (floating assumed flat over the hold).
Loan term used to compute the EMI.
Extra principal paid each year-end (shortens tenure, EMI fixed). Default 0.
Your top income-tax slab — applied to taxable rental income (and to the loss set-off shield).
Long-term capital-gains rate on property sale (12.5%, no indexation, post-23-Jul-2024).
Long-term capital-gains rate on the equity benchmark, above the annual exemption.
Annual equity LTCG exemption (₹1.25 lakh).
Assumed nominal return of the equity benchmark (Nifty/Sensex ~11%). The opportunity cost of buying property.
Consumer inflation, used only to express the terminal value in today's money.
Exit brokerage + legal as a share of the sale price.
Discount to clear the sale (plots are less liquid than flats), applied only at exit.
Credit saved rent for self-occupied (default off).
Nominal structure life (years), informs salvage.
One-off land-value uplifts {year, pct}.
TenantPaysCAM or OwnerBearsAll.
Self-occupied analogue: maintenance as % of property value.
Annual growth in the water/borewell charge.
Effective-rate toggle: none / 31.2% / 35.8%.
Mumbai apartments: model a redevelopment option value.
Age at which redevelopment becomes likely (years).
Redevelopment option value as % of land.
CompositeLoan (land+construction) or OwnFunds.
Interest rate on the land loan.
Interest-only on disbursed tranches during the build.
